key_insight: Capillary thinning is not just a way to measure one relaxation time; it is a benchmark complex flow that reveals how polymer conformation, geometry, and protocol jointly control viscoelastic response.
engineering_relevance: A Pipkin-diagram view of capillary thinning can help engineers compare constitutive models and build more reliable master curves for extension-dominated polymer flows.
method: Recast capillary-breakup rheometry output as a self-selected elastic strain rate using an elastic Weissenberg number Wi_e.
Organize results in an elastocapillary Pipkin diagram: Wi_e versus a geometry-controlled Deborah number.
Use mid-filament stress balance and scaling analysis to collapse family curves onto a master curve; compare C2D2 against FENE-P.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a model/data collapse spanning decades in molecular weight and concentration across multiple devices, with C2D2 capturing trends that the classical FENE-P model cannot.
watch_item: The framework depends on geometry-controlled and onset-referenced parameters, so careful experimental alignment and estimation of initial prestretch are important for consistent use.
paper_title: To win, a model must thin: Capillary thinning as a benchmark complex flow for constitutive models of viscoelastic polymer solutions
